place_results.title: CVS
place_results.description: Drugstore chain selling a variety of beauty & health products, plus some grocery & household items.

places.title: MoneyGram
places.address: ON THE CORNER OF, 298 MULBERRY STREET, E Houston St, New York, NY 10012

summary.snippet: "Staff are very friendly, shelves are always well stocked."
"You can see by the receipt: 1 set of vitamins was “ORIGINAL PRICE.”"
"It's a good place to get personal care items and groceries."

most_relevant.description: The pharmacist is consistently rude to me and to others I know -- it has become a miserable experience and I absolutely dread going there to pick up prescriptions. I have started using Duane Reade instead (where they actually show human decency), but they are closed on weekends which means sometimes I have to go to CVS for emergencies. I am starting to think that I'd be better off not having medication during a health crisis than interacting with this pharmacist. Truly, spare yourselves the trouble and use ANY OTHER pharmacy.

This is a very convenient store in a busy intersection that is always well stocked, always clean, and run by staff who are super friendly.
I walked through each and every aisle and quickly noticed that there were helpful signs posted and the shelves were full with a wide variety of everyday items.
I only wish they would designate at least one of their four automatic checkout counters to accept cash like their other store in Union Square, and also the store on Park Avenue/22nd street do. This is because it’s usually very busy at this location and not everyone pays with a credit card and many a time I have noticed at least two, and sometimes all of the automatic checkout counters vacant but five customers waiting in line to pay cash, because the vacant checkout counters only accept credit/debit cards. And then many a time there is only one cashier handling customers who pay cash. If any of those customers in line paying cash are doing a return or has other transaction-related issues, the rest of the cash paying customers will have to wait on line for a significant amount of time. I wish they would fix this issue  but I don’t think they will as I did bring it up and was told that they will not.
I’ve also noticed that far too many of their items are priced significantly higher than the competition. They are also not very good at being on top of restocking the protein shakes in the refrigerators although some of the popular shakes sell out quickly, leaving an empty slot in the fridge for days on end. The issues mentioned here apply only to this particular location.

This was an absolutely terrible experience. I had a vaccination appointment scheduled for 9:00 AM, yet the pharmacy didn’t even open until 9:15, and I only received the shot at 9:30. This shows a complete lack of respect for patients’ time and total disorganization. Scheduling appointments that you clearly cannot honor is unacceptable. Extremely unprofessional service. I strongly do not recommend this place for vaccinations.

Had a 1:00 minute clinic appointment for a shot. My name wasn’t called until 1:30 and at that point I had to go back to work. I was told that they “double book” appointments and that I was double booked with a 40 minute appointment which no one told me. The associate who was receiving people passed me twice and could have relayed the message, that it would be a minute and that if I was in a rush I probably should reschedule. Instead I was explained the situation in a snippy/sarcastic manner. Even if double booking is protocol, to not apprise someone who has been waiting there for 30 minutes past their appointment time despite the opportunity seems bonkers and a complete dereliction of professionalism or care.

Pharmacist (or maybe not a pharmacist?) behind the counter extremely annoyed by my questions about Sudafed I needed to purchase behind the counter as per my doctor’s instructions, and in the end, she told me to get the wrong dose/strength because she didn’t want to deal. Treated me like my questions were stupid. Now I have the wrong medication.

The staff working at the pharmacy are beyond rude and incredibly unhelpful. I know most pharmacies these days are terrible but this one is below average in a sea off bad options. If only there was a closer option.

I shop at this store often, but most of the time the staff is very rude. No smile, no customer service, and their attitude is really bad.
On October 15 around 10:30 PM, the Indian lady working there was extremely rude. I had a lot of items, and instead of helping, she asked me, “Do you know the price?” — what kind of question is that?
Please hire nicer, more professional, and more knowledgeable staff. This store really needs better customer service!!!  Like so so horrible!

I had a very disappointing experience at this store. The Indian lady with short brown ponytail hair working on October 2nd at around 3:00 pm showed no smile, no courtesy, and a lot of attitude. When I asked her several times to check my order, she kept insisting it was canceled. I explained that I never received a cancellation email and asked her to check again. She looked visibly annoyed, gave me a side look, and only then finally found my order.I also noticed she was not welcoming with other customers either — no greeting, no thank you, and no basic customer service skills. On top of that, the service was very slow. With this kind of attitude, it seems like she doesn’t even even want to work,better to stay home than treat customers this way. Honestly, I don’t know how someone with no professionalism got hired here. This was my first and last visit to this location, and with service like this, no one will want to return. I don’t even want to give one star.

live_hash.time_spent: People typically spend 10 min to 3.5 hr here
